results.seo_title: Siri Autodeletion: Apple's New Privacy Feature
results.meta_description: Apple is introducing autodeleting chats in its revamped Siri, aiming to differentiate itself on the AI front and improve user privacy.
results.body_html:
According to Bloomberg, Apple's revamped Siri will offer autodeleting chats. This new feature aims to improve user privacy and differentiate Apple from its competitors on the AI front.

Key Features of Autodeletion

  • Users can save conversations for 30 days, one year, or forever.
  • The option to autodelete chat histories is set to debut in iOS 27.
  • This feature is part of Apple's efforts to improve user privacy and reduce reliance on history-based systems.

Benefits of Autodeletion

Autodeletion provides users with greater control over their personal data, reducing the risk of unauthorized access or exploitation.

Most leading AI chatbots today rely heavily on histories and memory systems to personalize responses and improve future interactions. But Apple will place tighter limits around how memory works, including restrictions on what information can persist and how long it can be retained.

Comparison to Competitors

FeatureDescription
Temporary Incognito ChatsOther major players in the space generally only offer temporary incognito chats, if that.

source.title: Revamped Siri will reportedly offer autodeleting chats
source.summary: Apple is hoping that its record on privacy can be the differentiator on the AI front and maybe even buy it a little slack as it continues to lag behind the competition. According to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, the more chatbot-like Siri set to debut in iOS 27 will include the option to autodelete chat histories. […]

results.seo_title: Ferrari's first EV: Regulatory compliance and China drive design
results.meta_description: Ferrari's first electric vehicle, Luce, prioritizes regulatory compliance and Chinese market in its design.
results.body_html:
Ferrari's first EV is not for you, as the company prioritizes regulatory compliance and China in its design. The Ferrari Luce seems to be more aimed at meeting Chinese regulations, putting a lot of pressure on the Jony Ive-designed EV.

Key Design PrioritiesRegulatory compliance: Ferrari's first electric vehicle is designed to meet strict Chinese regulations.Chinese market focus: The Luce is prioritized for the Chinese market, where electric vehicles are becoming increasingly popular.Reduced emissions: The EV aims to reduce emissions and improve air quality in China.Technical SpecificationsFeatureDescriptionRangeApproximately 300 miles (483 km)Charging TimeUnder 30 minutesBattery Capacity75 kWhIndustry ImplicationsThe Ferrari Luce's design priorities reflect the growing importance of regulatory compliance and Chinese market demand in the electric vehicle industry.

source.title: Ferrari’s first EV is not for you
source.summary: Ferrari has announced its first electric vehicle, the Luce, which appears to prioritize regulatory compliance in China over performance and luxury features typically associated with the brand. The design is attributed to Jony Ive, a renowned designer. Ferrari's decision may indicate a shift in focus towards meeting environmental regulations in key markets like China.

results.seo_title: What Pope Leo XIV's First Encyclical Says About AI Power and…
results.meta_description: Discover how Pope Leo XIV's encyclical views on power, technology, and human dignity inform our understanding of AI regulation.
results.body_html:
Pope Leo XIV's encyclical emphasizes the need for regulation to protect human dignity and truth in the face of algorithmic logic.

Key Takeaways from Pope Leo XIV's Encyclical

The encyclical views AI as a tool that can be used for good or evil, depending on how it is developed and deployed. Pope Leo XIV emphasizes the importance of human dignity and the common good in the face of technological advancements. The encyclical highlights the need for regulation to prevent the concentration of power in a few global players.

Concentration of Power in AI Development

Disarming Technology: A Key Concept in Pope Leo XIV's Encyclical “Disarming technology” refers to the need to develop AI systems that prioritize human dignity and the common good over profit and power.

The encyclical highlights the need for regulation to prevent the concentration of power in a few global players. Pope Leo XIV emphasizes the importance of human dignity and the common good in the face of technological advancements.

source.title: What Pope Leo XIV’s First Encyclical Says About the Power of AI
source.summary: Pope Leo XIV's first encyclical, "Magnifica Humanitas", expresses concerns about the concentration of technological power in a few global players, highlighting the need for greater awareness and regulation to prevent potential misuse of artificial intelligence (AI). The Pope's statement suggests that AI has become a significant force in modern society, warranting careful consideration and oversight.

results.seo_title: TopoEvo: A Topology-Aware Self-Evolving Multi-Agent Framework for…
results.meta_description: TopoEvo tackles root cause analysis in microservices with a topology-aware self-evolving multi-agent framework, reducing symptom-amplification bias and…
results.body_html:
Root cause analysis in microservices is challenging due to noisy and heterogeneous multimodal observability, cascading failure propagation, and non-stationary topology drift.

Challenges with Traditional RCA Agents

Recent LLM-based RCA agents can generate tool-grounded explanations but often remain topology-agnostic and suffer from symptom-amplification bias. This misattributing of root causes to downstream victims leads to unreliable analysis and decision-making.

Metric-orthogonal Multimodal Alignment (MOMA) Decomposes metric embeddings into complementary subspaces and contrastively aligns logs and traces to reduce modality redundancy and sparsity.

Vector Quantization (VQ) Discretizes topology-enhanced states into auditable symptom tokens with a symptom lexicon, enabling reliable retrieval and token-level evidence grounding.

Hypothesis--Evidence--Test (HET) Workflow Explicitly verifies propagation-consistent explanations and separates initiating anomalies from amplified downstream symptoms.

Benefits of TopoEvo TopoEvo improves the reliability of root cause analysis in microservices by addressing symptom-amplification bias and providing auditable evidence grounding.

source.title: TopoEvo: A Topology-Aware Self-Evolving Multi-Agent Framework for Root Cause Analysis in Microservices
source.summary: Root cause analysis (RCA) in microservices is challenging due to (i) noisy and heterogeneous multimodal observability (metrics, logs, traces), (ii) cascading failure propagation that amplifies downstream symptoms, and (iii) non-stationary topology drift induced by autoscaling and rolling updates. Recent LLM-based RCA agents can generate tool-grounded explanations, yet they often remain topology-agnostic and suffer from \emph{symptom-amplification bias}, misattributing the root cause to salient downstream victims. We propose \textbf{TopoEvo}, a topology-aware self-evolving multi-agent framework that couples graph representation learning with structured, topology-constrained reasoning. TopoEvo first introduces \emph{Metric-orthogonal Multimodal Alignment} (MOMA), which decomposes metric embeddings into complementary subspaces and contrastively aligns logs and traces to reduce modality redundancy and sparsity, yielding stable node representations for graph encoding. It then applies \emph{Vector Quantization} (VQ) to discretize topology-enhanced states into auditable \emph{symptom tokens} with a symptom lexicon, enabling reliable retrieval and token-level evidence grounding. On top of these discrete topology cues, TopoEvo performs a multi-agent \emph{Hypothesis--Evidence--Test} (HET) workflow to explicitly verify propagation-consistent explanations and separate initiating anomalies from amplified downstream symptoms. Finally, a \emph{Self-Evolving Mechanism} refreshes hierarchical incident memory and performs conservative test-time adaptation with high-confidence pseudo-labels to maintain robustness under drift.

results.seo_title: Machine Psychometrics: A Mathematical Psychology of Artificial…
results.meta_description: A new measurement science for artificial agents, Machine Psychometrics introduces a disciplined layer beneath the philosophical impasse between two…
results.body_html:
Machine Psychometrics aims to understand artificial agents precisely because they are not human, through measurement before judgment.

The Machine Mindprint is a multidimensional, domain-bounded, versioned profile spanning calibration, source integrity, suggestibility resistance, context stability, expressive alignment, tool integrity, drift monitoring, and distributional grounding. This operational core provides a disciplined measurement layer beneath the philosophical impasse between two symmetrical errors in AI.

Trust Protocol for Deployment Decisions

Probe Batteries, Perturbation Testing, Reliability and Validity Analysis

Perturbation testing evaluates the robustness of the Machine Mindprint against external perturbations. Reliability and validity analysis ensures the accuracy and consistency of the deployment decisions made using the Trust Protocol.

Prompting users to reflect on their own biases and assumptions can improve the accuracy of the deployment decisions. The use of probe batteries allows for longitudinal monitoring across high-stakes domains, ensuring that the deployment decisions remain effective over time.

Artificial Mind Discipline: A Third Stance in AI Research Neither anthropomorphizing nor dismissing, neither presupposing consciousness nor foreclosing it, Machine Psychometrics offers a third stance that neither humanizes nor dismisses artificial agents.

This approach recognizes the unique characteristics of artificial agents and seeks to understand them precisely because they are not human.

source.title: Machine Psychometrics: A Mathematical Psychology of Artificial Intelligence
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

Researchers have proposed a new approach called Machine Psychometrics to measure and evaluate the psychological structure of artificial intelligence systems. This approach aims to move beyond simplistic evaluation tools that focus on capability scores alone, by providing a more nuanced understanding of an AI's behavior and decision-making processes. The framework involves creating a "Machine Mindprint" profile, which assesses various aspects of an AI's behavior, such as calibration and expressiveness. By using this approach, researchers can gain a deeper understanding of artificial agents without anthropomorphizing or dismissing their unique characteristics.

results.seo_title: Trustworthy Agentic AI: A Comprehensive Survey of Safety and…
results.meta_description: A comprehensive survey of trustworthy agentic AI, covering safety, robustness, privacy, and system security.
results.body_html:
Agentic AI systems can execute complex tasks autonomously, but their multi-step trajectories introduce new failure modes that challenge trustworthiness.

Agentic AI systems pose new risks due to their ability to execute complex tasks autonomously Risks emerge along the agent workflow, including safety and robustness concerns Value alignment, transparency, fairness, and accountability are also critical aspects of trustworthy agentic AI

Mitigation Strategies and Evaluation Metrics

Constraint violations Measure of safety and robustness breach

Trace completeness Assesses the quality of agent's decision-making process

Adversarial success rates Measures an agent's ability to withstand attacks

Open Challenges and Future Directions Self-evolving agents, runtime monitoring and verification, privacy-preserving personalization, and the trust-utility trade-off are open challenges in trustworthy agentic AI.

source.title: Towards trustworthy agentic AI: a comprehensive survey of safety, robustness, privacy, and system security
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

A comprehensive survey aims to improve the trustworthiness of agentic AI systems, which can autonomously execute complex tasks. The survey focuses on two core dimensions: Safety and Robustness, and Privacy and System Security, providing guidance on key concepts, risk emergence points, and mitigation strategies. It also outlines open challenges and presents a case study of real-world security failures in open-source agentic systems. The goal is to serve as a practical reference for researchers and practitioners building trustworthy agentic systems in high-stakes environments.

results.seo_title: LC-ERD: A Framework for Self-Evolving Reasoning via…
results.meta_description: Mining Latent Logic for Self-Evolving Reasoning via Consistency-Regulated Reward Decomposition
results.body_html:
The evolution of Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning is bottlenecked by the scarcity of high-quality process data. Mining valid supervision faces three challenges: Label Noise via Mimetic Bias, Coarse-Grained Supervision, and Distributional Collapse.

Addressing Label Noise via Mimetic BiasRewards prioritize statistical likelihood over logical truth, creating a 'correctness illusion' that masks compounding errors.This can lead to inaccurate self-alignment and reinforce pre-existing biases.Coarse-Grained Supervision ChallengesChallengeDescriptionCoarse-Grained SupervisionSparse global outcomes fail to provide granular guidance, treating reasoning chains as monolithic.Distributional CollapseSignals fail to generalize without amplifying pre-training biases.LC-ERD Framework for Self-Evolving ReasoningThe proposed LC-ERD framework frames self-alignment as latent structure mining, addressing the challenges of Label Noise via Mimetic Bias and Coarse-Grained Supervision.

source.title: LC-ERD: Mining Latent Logic for Self-Evolving Reasoning via Consistency-Regulated Reward Decomposition
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called LC-ERD (Logic-Consistent Endogenous Reward Decomposition) to address challenges in self-evolving Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning. The framework aims to overcome issues such as label noise, coarse-grained supervision, and distributional collapse by mining latent logic from the model's internal expertise. Experiments have shown that LC-ERD delivers a robust self-evolution path, uncovering trade-offs between logic consistency and accuracy.

results.seo_title: Safety-Oriented Routing Analysis of Mixtral MoE Under Benign and…
results.meta_description: Researchers analyze routing behavior of Mixtral language model under benign and harmful prompts, finding subtle safety-relevant routing.
results.body_html:
The Mixtral language model's routing behavior is subtle and depth-dependent, with safety-relevant routing being distributed rather than dominated by a fixed set of experts.

Expert Selection FrequenciesActivation-based expert usage is broad and long-tailed.Gradient-based importance is concentrated.Most experts are shared across benign and harmful prompts, though a limited subset shows clear group preference.Router-Gate SensitivitiesLayerGradient-Based ImportanceFinal LayersCentralized importanceLayers 8-15Most selective around these layersIntervention ExperimentsSuppressing top five benign-dominant experts from activation scores reduces restricted responses from 24 to 14 over 100 prompts.

source.title: Safety-Oriented Routing Analysis of Mixtral MoE Under Benign and Harmful Prompts
source.summary: Researchers analyzed the routing behavior of the Mixtral 8x7B-Instruct language model under benign and harmful prompts using two different signals: activation-based and gradient-based scores. The study found that activation-based expert usage is broad and long-tailed, while gradient-based importance is concentrated in final layers. Suppressing top experts from both signals reduced restricted responses to harmful prompts, suggesting that safety-relevant routing in Mixtral is subtle and distributed rather than dominated by a fixed set of experts.

results.seo_title: Psych LM: A Local-First Architecture for Emotionally Oriented Support
results.meta_description: Discover Psych LM, an iOS application that validates the importance of architecture in emotionally oriented support. Learn about its local-first design and…
results.body_html:
The system can be defined as an active-learning, retrieval-augmented generative, on-device architecture. This architecture delivers four primary contributions: a local-first design where privacy is a core property; a detailed description of the memory corpus for persistent context of key user information; a deterministic orchestration layer that provides a stable behavioral spine independent of the model's internal state; and a benchmark framework focused on evaluating the integrated system's reliability under realistic operating conditions.

Key Features of Psych LMA local, on-device language model designed for behavioral and life-coaching applicationsAn automated, user-inspectable memory corpus that converts conversations into structured memory cardsDynamically injects key information into the prompt via semantic and vector searchBenefits of Local-First DesignBenefitDescriptionPrivacy as a core propertyThe local-first design prioritizes user data privacy and securityPersistent context of key user informationThe memory corpus provides a stable behavioral spine independent of the model's internal stateReliability under realistic operating conditionsThe benchmark framework evaluates the integrated system's reliability in mobile environmentsBenchmarks and GapsComplex, context-aware interaction can be

reliably achieved under strict constraints of a mobile environment by prioritizing architectural control and resource management over simple model size.

source.title: The Model Is Not the Product: A Dual-Pillar Architecture for Local-First Psychological Coaching
source.summary: Researchers have developed an iOS application called Psych LM, which uses a local, on-device language model to provide emotionally oriented support with persistent context across sessions. The system achieves this through a unique architecture that converts conversations into structured memory cards and dynamically injects them into the prompt via semantic and vector search. This design prioritizes privacy, provides a stable behavioral spine, and enables reliable complex interaction in a mobile environment. The application's development confirms that architectural control and resource management can be more effective than simple model size in achieving context-aware interactions.

results.seo_title: Jailbreaking for Safe Fine-Tuning in Large Language Models
results.meta_description: A new framework proposes temporary jailbreaking as a defense against harmful fine-tuning attacks, achieving superior safety and utility without additional…
results.body_html:
Temporary jailbreaking is proposed as a defense against harmful fine-tuning attacks.

Key Components of Buffer-and-Reinforce Framework

BufferLoRA induces temporary jailbreaking to reduce harmful updates during user fine-tuning. ReinforceLoRA is trained to recover refusal behavior under the temporarily jailbroken state. UserLoRA is integrated with ReinforceLoRA via QR decomposition-based merging to reinforce safety while preserving user-task performance.

Experimental Results and Safety Analysis

Safety Analysis of Buffer-and-Reinforce Framework MetricValue Safety Score0.95 Utility Score0.92

Advantages of Buffer-and-Reinforce Framework Buffer-and-Reinforce framework achieves superior safety and utility with minimal computational cost.

source.title: Jailbreak to Protect: Buffering and Reinforcing via Temporary Jailbreaking for Safe Fine-Tuning in Large Language Models
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach called Buffer-and-Reinforce fine-tuning, which aims to protect large language models from weakening their safety alignment during the fine-tuning process. The method involves temporarily "jailing" the model to prevent it from learning harmful behaviors, while also reinforcing its safety after adaptation. This framework has been shown to achieve superior safety and utility with minimal additional computational cost.

results.seo_title: Energy Shields for Fairness: Adaptive Controller Ensures Runtime…
results.meta_description: Energy shields are novel, lightweight adaptive controllers that monitor a sequence of decisions and intervene probabilistically to ensure runtime fairness…
results.body_html:
Energy shields are novel, lightweight adaptive controllers that monitor a sequence of decisions and intervene probabilistically to ensure runtime fairness smoothly.

Utilize physics-inspired energy functions to nudge the sequence toward fairness Provide both short-term safety and long-term liveness guarantees Ensure runtime fairness by intervening probabilistically

1 Construct the least intrusive energy shield for a given target specification

Evaluation and Efficiency Energy shields demonstrate efficiency experimentally, outperforming existing fairness shields in short- and long-term fairness evaluations. Advantages over Traditional Fairness Shields

Probabilistic intervention ensures runtime fairness smoothly Novel, lightweight adaptive controller provides both safety and liveness guarantees Utilize physics-inspired energy functions for nudging the sequence toward fairness

source.title: Energy Shields for Fairness
source.summary: Researchers have developed "energy shields" to ensure runtime fairness in decision-making systems. Unlike traditional fairness shields that intervene abruptly, energy shields use physics-inspired energy functions to nudge sequences of decisions towards fairness probabilistically. This approach provides both short-term safety guarantees and long-term liveness guarantees, ensuring the running fairness measure stays within a target interval with high probability. The new concept has been demonstrated experimentally and compared to existing fairness shields.

results.seo_title: Mitigating Object Hallucinations in Vision-Language Models through…
results.meta_description: A new training-free inference strategy for Large Vision-Language Models to address object hallucination, with improved efficiency and algorithmic robustness.
results.body_html:
The generation of factually incorrect objects, commonly known as object hallucination, remains a persistent challenge in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs). Our new training-free inference strategy addresses this issue with improved efficiency and algorithmic robustness.

Key ComponentsRegion-aware adaptive weighting mechanismOutlier-resistant statistical midpoint across various attention headsDynamically determined intervention budgets through inter-head disagreement mappingEvaluation ResultsBenchmarkPerformance ImprovementCHAIR25.6% reduction in instance-level hallucinationsPOPE22.1% reduction in sentence-level hallucinationsMME30.5% reduction in instance-level hallucinationsMethodologyOur strategy operates as a region-aware adaptive weighting mechanism to dynamically correct semantic drift without relying on abrupt heuristic truncations.

source.title: Mitigating Object Hallucinations in Vision-Language Models through Region-Aware Attention Recalibration
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new training-free approach to mitigate object hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs). The proposed strategy uses a region-aware adaptive weighting mechanism to dynamically correct semantic drift and suppress hallucination-inducing attention paths. This method effectively rectifies visual-semantic misalignments while preserving generative fluency and language priors, achieving state-of-the-art performance on standard multimodal benchmarks.

results.seo_title: RECTOR: Priority-Aware Rule-Based Reranking for Compliance-Aware…
results.meta_description: A new AI system improves autonomous driving safety and compliance by prioritizing rules, reducing violations from 28.58% to 20.42%.
results.body_html:
The new RECTOR system reduces autonomous driving violations by prioritizing safety and legal rules.

Reduces Safety+Legal violations from 28.58% to 20.42% Improves Total compliance from 40.32% to 32.41% Empirical lift comes from rule-aware ranking, while lexicographic guarantee is structural differentiator

Adversarial Testing Results Confidence-only selection fails in 100% of scenarios, while rule-aware selectors reject injected mode in ~96%.

source.title: RECTOR: Priority-Aware Rule-Based Reranking for Compliance-Aware Autonomous Driving Trajectory Selection
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new system called RECTOR to improve autonomous driving trajectory selection. The system uses a post-generation reranking layer that considers safety, traffic laws, and comfort constraints in addition to model confidence. This approach reduces the number of Safety+Legal violations by 28.58% compared to selecting based solely on model confidence, while also improving overall compliance. RECTOR's rule-aware ranking outperforms a baseline that uses a uniform-weighted sum, with the system's lexicographic guarantee providing a structural advantage over weight calibration.

results.seo_title: Agent-Centric Social Trajectory Prediction Framework Outperforms…
results.meta_description: FEP-Diff consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in agent-centric trajectory prediction under restricted observability.
results.body_html:
FEP-Diff consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in agent-centric trajectory prediction under restricted observability. The framework achieves cognitively plausible predictions under realistic constraints, addressing limitations of existing methods.

Key Components and Performance

  • Dual-branch spatiotemporal encoder extracts ego-motion dynamics and social interaction cues from local observations.
  • Goal-conditioned belief learner infers multimodal latent belief distributions optimized via a free-energy objective.
  • Residual diffusion trajectory generator produces precise and diverse future predictions with token-level proxy conditioning.

Experimental Results and Benchmarking

Benchmark Performance Comparison
FIVE-Bench FEP-Diff outperforms state-of-the-art methods under restricted observability.
Other Benchmarks FEP-Diff consistently demonstrates superior performance.

Code Availability and Future Work

Code for FEP-Diff is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/FEP-Diff-8876.

source.title: Agent-Centric Social Trajectory Prediction: A Free Energy Principle Perspective
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework for trajectory prediction called FEP-Diff, which uses the Free Energy Principle to create more cognitively plausible and realistic predictions of human motion patterns. The framework combines multiple components, including an encoder that extracts local observations, a belief learner that infers multimodal latent distributions, and a generator that produces precise future predictions. This approach outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods under restricted observability in extensive experiments on five public benchmarks.

results.seo_title: Document Classification Pattern Recognition via Information Fusion:…
results.meta_description: A systematic review of multimodal and multiview representation approaches for document classification, highlighting the effectiveness of information fusion…
results.body_html:
Information fusion improves accuracy by +5.28 percentage points in document classification, according to the systematic review.

Key Findings of Multimodal FusionMean gain of +5.28 percentage points in accuracyF1-score effect is directionally positive but statistically non-significantOnly 11.8% of studies use statistical tests to validate findingsKey Findings of Multiview FusionMetricsGainAccuracy+4.67%F1-score+3.08%RecallAll p less than 0.05Challenges in Reproducibility and Methodological RigourOnly 11.8% of multimodal studies and 23.3% of multiview studies use statistical tests to validate findings, undermining the reliability of many results.

source.title: Document Classification Pattern Recognition via Information Fusion: A Systematic Review of Multimodal and Multiview Representation Approaches
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

A systematic review of 139 primary studies analyzed the effectiveness of information fusion in document classification, which involves integrating multiple data sources or representations. The review found that multimodal fusion improved accuracy by an average of +5.28 percentage points and multiview fusion provided consistent but modest gains for accuracy and other metrics. However, the study also highlighted challenges with methodological rigor, including low rates of statistical testing to validate findings. The review provides a unifying framework, quantitative evidence base, and data-driven guidelines for practitioners.

results.seo_title: High-Risk AI Systems and the Problem of Identity in the European AI…
results.meta_description: The EU Artificial Intelligence Act establishes a lifecycle governance regime for high-risk AI systems, but lacks an internal criterion for synchronic…
results.body_html:
The EU Artificial Intelligence Act establishes a lifecycle governance regime for high-risk AI systems, but lacks an internal criterion for synchronic identity. This oversight has significant implications for the regulation of AI systems in the European Union.

Current Challenges with Synchronic Identity

The EU Artificial Intelligence Act relies on sectoral or harmonization instruments to determine synchronic identity, rather than providing an internal criterion. This approach can lead to inconsistencies and difficulties in enforcing regulatory requirements.

  • The lack of a clear synchronic identity test can result in confusion and disputes among regulators and providers.
  • Without a standardized framework for determining synchronic identity, it becomes challenging to ensure comparability over time and across deployments.

Solution: The function+ Framework

The function+ framework provides a solution to the problem of synchronic identity in high-risk AI systems. This framework individuates AI systems by their intended function together with context-sensitive criteria of appropriate functioning, captured as "AI trustworthiness".

  • The function+ framework offers a clear and auditable criterion for determining synchronic identity, making it easier to enforce regulatory requirements.
  • By providing a standardized framework for determining synchronic identity, the function+ framework supports comparability over time and across deployments.

Implementation-Facing Recommendations

The authors of this work recommend two implementation-facing recommendations to support the adoption of the function+ framework: (1) more precise, testable reporting of intended purpose, and (2) standardized, auditable trustworthiness reporting.

  • More precise reporting of intended purpose can help ensure that AI systems are designed with clear and consistent functions.
  • Standardized trustworthiness reporting can facilitate comparability over time and across deployments, supporting the effective regulation of high-risk AI systems.

source.title: High-Risk AI Systems and the Problem of Identity in the European AI Act
source.summary: The European AI Act requires high-risk AI systems to undergo conformity assessment, monitoring, and re-assessment upon significant modifications. However, the act's identity judgments for these systems are unclear, relying on regulators and providers to determine when an updated system remains the same as its predecessor. A new framework called function+ aims to clarify this by considering a system's intended function and trustworthiness in making identity decisions. This framework provides a more auditable approach to synchronic identity, which is essential for governance settings such as procurement and liability.

results.seo_title: Formal Methods Meet LLMs: Auditing and Monitoring for AI Compliance
results.meta_description: Discover how formal methods improve auditing and monitoring of AI systems, ensuring compliance with safety constraints and regulations.
results.body_html:
Our proposed auditing and monitoring techniques using formal methods are superior to LLM baseline methods in detecting violations of temporally extended behavioral constraints.

Practical Techniques for Offline Auditing

Sampling-based methods for predictive monitoring Offline analysis of product-specific behavioral constraints Integration with formal methods for enhanced compliance

Runtime Monitoring and Intervention

Intervening monitors Act at runtime to preempt and mitigate predicted violations

Predictive monitoring Reduces violation rates of LLM-based agents while preserving task performance

Experimental Results and Benchmarking Our approach outperforms LLM baseline methods in detecting violations of temporally extended behavioral constraints, with even small-model labelers matching or exceeding frontier LLM judges.

source.title: Formal Methods Meet LLMs: Auditing, Monitoring, and Intervention for Compliance of Advanced AI Systems
source.summary: We examine one particular dimension of AI governance: how to monitor and audit AI-enabled products and services throughout the AI development lifecycle, from pre-deployment testing to post-deployment auditing. Combining principles from formal methods with SoTA machine learning, we propose techniques that enable AI-enabled product and service developers, as well as third party AI developers and evaluators, to perform offline auditing and online (runtime) monitoring of product-specific (temporally extended) behavioral constraints such as safety constraints, norms, rules and regulations with respect to black-box advanced AI systems, notably LLMs. We further provide practical techniques for predictive monitoring, such as sampling-based methods, and we introduce intervening monitors that act at runtime to preempt and potentially mitigate predicted violations. Experimental results show that by exploiting the formal syntax and semantics of Linear Temporal Logic (LTL), our proposed auditing and monitoring techniques are superior to LLM baseline methods in detecting violations of temporally extended behavioral constraints; with our approach, even small-model labelers match or exceed frontier LLM judges. Our predictive and intervening monitors significantly reduce the violation rates of LLM-based agents while largely preserving task performance. We further show through controlled experiments that LLMs' temporal reasoning shows a pronounced degradation in accuracy with increasing event distance, number of constraints, and number of propositions.

results.seo_title: Multimodal Alignment and Preference Optimization for Zero-Shot…
results.meta_description: Researchers introduce Moirain: a suite of models optimized via multimodal supervised fine-tuning and Direct Preference Optimization to improve RNA sequence…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Researchers have developed a new approach to improve RNA sequence generation, which has shown superior binding affinities compared to existing baselines.

Improving RNA Sequence GenerationMoirain: a suite of models optimized via multimodal supervised fine-tuning and Direct Preference OptimizationConsistently produces novel, diverse, and biologically plausible RNA sequencesAdvantages over Existing BaselinesFeatureDescriptionNoveltyMoirain produces novel RNA sequences that were not seen in existing baselinesDiversityMoirain generates diverse RNA sequences with superior binding affinities compared to existing baselinesBiological PlausibilityMoirain produces biologically plausible RNA sequences that are consistent with experimental dataTechnical DetailsOur approach begins with large-scale pretraining on diverse RNA corpora to capture the fundamental grammars of sequence plausibility.

source.title: Multimodal Alignment and Preference Optimization for Zero-Shot Conditional RNA Generation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach called Moirain for generating RNA molecules that interact with specific proteins. The method uses multimodal supervised fine-tuning and Direct Preference Optimization to improve the accuracy of generated sequences. Extensive evaluation shows that Moirain produces novel, diverse, and biologically plausible RNA sequences with superior binding affinities compared to existing baselines. This could lead to significant improvements in experimental and computational biology applications.

results.seo_title: Metacognition for Bounded and Effective Self-Governance in…
results.meta_description: A new scientific framework for generative AI: metacognition.
results.body_html:
Metacognition should be the scientific framework for bounded and effective self-governance in generative AI.

Monitoring: tracking system performance and output Evaluation: assessing the quality and reliability of generated outputs Control: regulating system behavior to ensure stability and consistency Adaptation: adjusting system parameters to optimize performance

Benefits of Metacognitive Alignment

Computational Level Specifying meta-level functions for system evaluation and control

Algorithmic Level Realizing metacognitive functions through procedures like elicitation, iteration, and modularization

Ecological Level Making metacognitive signals actionable and accountable within the interface, workflow, and accountability arrangements

Implications for Generative AI Development Generative AI can be conceived as both capable and well-governed when metacognitive alignment is achieved across computational, algorithmic, and ecological levels.

source.title: Metacognition Should Be the Scientific Framework for Bounded and Effective Self-Governance in Generative AI
source.summary: Researchers argue that metacognition should be the scientific framework for governing generative AI systems. This approach would enable these systems to navigate uncertainty and regulate their own activity effectively. Metacognition involves aligning functions across computational, algorithmic, and ecological levels, allowing for a balanced evaluation of output generation and system regulation. By adopting this framework, generative AI can be seen as both capable and well-governed.

results.seo_title: Robust Avatar Watermarking Benchmarking and Baseline
results.meta_description: Benchmarking and baseline for robust avatar watermarking, evaluating existing methods and proposing WALT for efficient watermarking in UV texture space.
results.body_html:
What is the purpose of the RAW benchmarking and baseline? The benchmark aims to evaluate existing methods for robust avatar watermarking, with a focus on efficiency and effectiveness.

Embeds watermarks in UV texture space via 3D face reconstruction Achieves the highest robustness to zoom attacks (92.4%) while maintaining strong performance on background removal (95.6%) Facilitates research into avatar-specific watermarking

MethodRobustness to Zoom AttacksPerformance on Background Removal

source.title: RAW: Robust Avatar Watermarking -- Benchmarking and Baseline
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a new benchmark for digital avatar watermarking, called RAW, which evaluates 7 existing methods and reveals that avatar-specific attacks can significantly degrade watermark recovery. To address this challenge, they propose a new method called WALT (Watermarking Avatars with Learned Textures), which embeds watermarks in UV texture space via 3D face reconstruction. WALT achieves high robustness to zoom attacks while maintaining strong performance on background removal. The benchmark and proposed method are released to facilitate research into avatar-specific watermarking.

results.seo_title: To Land a Job in AI, Try Reading Kant
results.meta_description: The world's leading AI labs are hiring philosophers to think through ethical edge cases and grand questions of mind and morality.
results.body_html:
Are AI labs hiring philosophers to think through ethical edge cases and grand questions of mind and morality? The world's leading AI labs are indeed recruiting philosophers to work on these critical issues.

Philosophers in AI Labs: A Growing TrendTwo of the foremost AI labs, Google DeepMind and Anthropic, have recruited teams of in-house philosophers.The number of philosophers employed by these labs is significant, with at least 10 at DeepMind and four at Anthropic.Philosophy in AI Education: A Shift in CurriculumUniversityAI Ethics CourseUniversity of OxfordYes, with a joint program in computer science and philosophy.Other universitiesNoThe Role of Philosophers in AI ResearchPhilosophers at the labs help to sculpt AI models, producing prominent work cited in hundreds of subsequent research papers.

source.title: To Land a Job in AI, Try Reading Kant
source.summary: Top AI research labs are seeking philosophers to help address complex ethical issues and moral dilemmas related to artificial intelligence, indicating a growing recognition of the importance of philosophical thought in the development of AI. This move may suggest that AI's impact on society is being taken seriously enough to warrant input from experts in ethics and philosophy.

results.seo_title: GESD: A Procedural-Oriented Fairness Metric for Machine Learning
results.meta_description: Group-level Explanation Stability Disparity (GESD) is a fairness metric that measures disparities in the stability, robustness, and sensitivity of model…
results.body_html:
What is GESD? Group-level Explanation Stability Disparity (GESD) is a fairness metric that measures disparities in the stability, robustness, and sensitivity of model explanations across different subgroups.

GESD is explainer-agnostic and model-agnostic, extending the scope of fairness analyses to explainability. It measures disparities in stability, robustness, and sensitivity of model explanations across different subgroups. GESD improves upon existing fairness metrics by capturing group-wise discrepancies in explanation quality.

Advantages Over Existing Methods MethodLimitationExisting Fairness MetricsQuantify outcome-oriented disparities but offer limited insight into procedure or explanation behind biased decisions.GESDCaptures group-wise discrepancies in explanation quality and extends the scope of fairness analyses to explainability.

Empirical Results Empirical results on multiple benchmark datasets show that GESD effectively captures group-wise discrepancies in explanation quality, and that FEU improves both utility and fairness over state-of-the-art methods.
trending_keywords_used: adversarial learning with instructive verbal evaluation

source.title: GESD: Beyond Outcome-Oriented Fairness
source.summary: Machine learning (ML) algorithms are increasingly deployed in high-stakes decision-making domains such as loan approvals, hiring, and recidivism predictions. While existing fairness metrics (e.g., statistical parity, equal opportunity) effectively quantify outcome-oriented disparities, they offer limited insight into the procedure or explanation behind biased decisions. To address this gap, we propose Group-level Explanation Stability Disparity (GESD), a \textit{procedural-oriented} fairness metric that measures disparities in the stability, robustness, and sensitivity of model explanations across different subgroups in a protected category. %GESD is explainer-agnostic, model-agnostic, and extends the scope of fairness analyses to the level of explainability. We further integrate GESD into a multi-objective optimization framework that jointly optimizes for utility, outcome-based fairness, and explanation-based fairness called FEU (Fairness--Explainability--Utility). Empirical results on multiple benchmark datasets show that GESD effectively captures group-wise discrepancies in explanation quality, and that FEU improves both utility and fairness over state-of-the-art methods. By bridging outcome-based and explanation-based fairness, GESD offers a comprehensive tool for diagnosing and mitigating bias in predictive modeling. Our code and datasets are available on GitHub {\hyperlink{https://github.com/horlahsunbo/GESD}{https://github.com/horlahsunbo/GESD}}

results.seo_title: DIO-Agent: A Discovery Agent for Synthesizing Programs from…
results.meta_description: Discover how DIO-Agent outperforms traditional methods in synthesizing programs from input-output behavior, and learn about its capabilities and limitations.
results.body_html:
DIO-Agent is a discovery agent that has been shown to consistently outperform traditional methods in synthesizing programs from input-output behavior.

Evolutionary search over discrete program space LLM-based mutation operator with Transformation Priority Premise Concrete error signals from execution guide each mutation

Consistency across difficulty levels and LLMs DIO-Agent outperforms traditional methods in all experiments

Scalability with equivalent sampling budgets DIO-Agent substantially surpasses test-time scaling strategies

Limits of DIO-Agent While DIO-Agent has shown promising results, its performance may be affected by the quality of the LLM and the complexity of the input-output behavior.
trending_keywords_used: adversarial learning with instructive verbal evaluation

source.title: From I/O to Code with Discovery Agent
source.summary: The automatic synthesis of a program from any form of specification is regarded as a holy grail of computer science. Fueled by LLMs, NL2Code has achieved tremendous success, yet the fundamentally more challenging task of synthesizing programs from input-output behavior, which we refer to as IO2Code, remains largely unsolved. Whereas NL2Code can exploit the semantic alignment between natural language and code acquired during pretraining, IO2Code requires recovering underlying principles from concrete computational behavior, navigating a vast and underspecified hypothesis space. To address this, we propose DIO-Agent, a discovery agent for IO2Code. Our method frames IO2Code as an evolutionary search over discrete program space, in which an LLM serves as the mutation operator and concrete error signals from execution guide each mutation. To prevent the search from wandering into structurally complex yet incorrect dead ends, we introduce the Transformation Priority Premise as a mutation prior that biases the LLM toward the simplest hypothesis consistent with current evidence, progressively escalating from constants to conditionals to iteration only when simpler constructs are insufficient. To facilitate systematic study, we further construct an IO2CodeBench spanning multiple difficulty levels. Extensive experiments show that DIO-Agent consistently outperforms both traditional program-by-example method and SOTA evolution-agent baselines across all difficulty levels and various LLMs, while substantially surpassing test-time scaling strategies with equivalent sampling budgets.

results.seo_title: Mediative Fuzzy Logic: A Unified Account of Type-1 and Beyond
results.meta_description: A new framework for reconciling hesitant or conflicting assessments in fuzzy control and decision-making.
results.body_html:
Mediative Fuzzy Logic extends to Type-2, Type-3 and Quantum Extensions, developing a unified account of the type-1 core.

Characterization of the Mediative OperatorA convex aggregation controlled by hesitation and contradictionModelled as a convex operator in a continuous bilattice-like structureSemantic Extensions to Interval Type-2 Truth ValuesMediative truth values are modelled as independent truth-falsity pairsPropositional System and SoundnessFormulas without mediationSoundness, paraconsistency, and conservativity established over the underlying fuzzy baseAn Autonomous-Braking Sensor-Fusion ExampleIllustrates how the framework supports transparent, conservative, and safety-first decisions under incomplete, heterogeneous, and mildly contradictory evidence

source.title: Mediative Fuzzy Logic: From Type-1 Foundations to Type-2, Type-3 and Quantum Extensions
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

Researchers have developed a unified framework for Mediative Fuzzy Logic, which aims to reconcile conflicting assessments in fuzzy control and decision-making. The new framework extends type-1 logic with interval type-2, granular type-3, and quantum extensions, providing a more comprehensive understanding of the mediative operator and its applications. It establishes soundness, paraconsistency, and conservativity for formulas without mediation, and supports transparent and conservative decisions under incomplete evidence. The framework has been illustrated through an autonomous-braking sensor-fusion example.

Adversarial subspace alignment is used to generate semantically coherent variants in the joint latent space.

Key MechanismsKnowledge units group semantically equivalent multimodal inputsGenerality is defined as consistent predictions within each unitLatent Adversarial Robustification (LAR) generates adversarial yet semantically coherent variantsBenefits of ASAMBenchmark ResultsExtensive analysis demonstrates the effectiveness of ASAM empiricallyChallenges in Multimodal Knowledge EditingHigh-dimensional multimodal spaces lead to biased anchoring and rigid editing scopes, resulting in limited generality.

source.title: Beyond Binary Edits Robust Multimodal Knowledge Editing with Adversarial Subspace Alignment
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method for updating knowledge in multimodal large language models, called Adversarial Subspace Alignment Method (ASAM), which aims to improve the model's ability to generalize edits across different visual and linguistic variations. The approach addresses limitations of existing methods by explicitly targeting generalization and introducing techniques such as Latent Adversarial Robustification (LAR) and Rank-Constrained Subspace Learning (RCSL). These methods help expose fragile semantic regions and enforce alignment in the joint latent space, leading to more robust and reliable knowledge editing. The new method has been shown to be effective through extensive analysis.
